methodology: Single-pass root-cause attribution using existing forensic modules and deterministic pipeline replays. No new instrumentation. Word-order checks use token-sequence equality (not set overlap). Timing checks use segment-0 and word-aligned anchors, not index-aligned cue[i] vs segment[i].

rtlRootCause.mechanism: All segment tokens are flattened into one array, then globally sorted by `start` (`out.sort((a, b) => a.start - b.start)`). Phrase text is built only from that sorted timeline in `composeRhythmBlocks` via `chunkMeta.map((w) => w.word).join(' ')` (line 643), not from `segment.text` reading order. Overlapping progressive ASR/translation segments (partial cue then growing full cue) each contribute a full linearized token run; after sort, tokens interleave and duplicate. RTL/ASS stages (`buildCueLines`, `linesToAssText`, `buildRtlDialogueText`) preserve the already-corrupted string order.
rtlRootCause.before: این کار حتی برای بهترین ورزشکارها هم سخته
rtlRootCause.after: کاری سخته هم ورزشکارها بهترین برای
rtlRootCause.userReportedObserved: کاری سخته هم ورزشکارها بهترین برای

firstCorruptedStringEmittedAt.note: First human-visible phrase string diverges from translated segment text here; upstream sort in normalizeWordTimeline is the first token-order mutation.

stagesVerifiedUnchanged: linesToAssText token emission order (matches phrase.text when tags stripped)

proof.exportStripsWords: export-video.js normalizeSegments maps only {start,end,text} — words never reach server export.

inputSegments.text: این کار حتی برای بهترین ورزشکارها هم سخته

progressiveOverlapReplay.firstPhraseText: این این کار حتی کار برای

nonMonotonicWordReplay.input: single segment with seg.words timestamps out of text order
nonMonotonicWordReplay.expectedTextOrder: این کار حتی برای بهترین ورزشکارها هم سخته

singleSegmentControl.input: one segment, full sentence, export-style (no words)

contributingUpstream: website/video-export/video-export.js getSourceTruthSegments() drops words and may send overlapping segment lists from translation/preview
Overlapping partial + full cues common in streaming/progressive transcription
notRootCause: libass BiDi alone (logical ASS string matches phrase.text before burn; corruption precedes ASS)

timingRootCause.mechanism: First spoken-word visibility at export is bounded below by `segments[0].start` already ≈1.79s at phrase-pipeline input. Post-input stages move ASS start earlier, not later.

attributionChain.segment0Start: provider-dependent; captured in WHISPER-STARTTIME-FORENSICS when job runs

attributionChain.note: First change in unit test when provider raw 0.5s → filtered 1.79s

firstCodeChangeOnSegment0Start.whenNoChangeAfterFirstSnapshot: No post-Whisper function in captured lineage changes segment 0 start; late first caption inherits provider/filter start (~1.79s), not phrase/ASS delay

notRootCause: composeRhythmBlocks adding multi-second delay to first cue (moves earlier via preroll)
applyVisualReadabilityWindows (renderStart moves earlier up to maxLeadExtensionSec)
index-aligned exportInput[i] vs phraseCue[i] comparisons (fake multi-second drift)

syncRootCause.mechanism: Word-aligned comparison for first 20 phrase cues: assVisibleTime ≤ phraseVisibleTime ≈ firstSpokenWordTime (small negative realSpeechDeltaMs from preroll). Phrase→ASS code does not introduce positive lag vs firstWordStart.
syncRootCause.realSpeechDeltaMsDefinition: assVisibleTime - firstSpokenWordTime (positive = caption late vs speech anchor)

conditionalCodeLag.condition: Only when driftCorrectionApplied > 0 (rolling correction adds to block.start)
conditionalCodeLag.note: Not observed in standard replay; can shift cues later when active

conditionalBurnLag.note: Post-ASS; check render-diagnostics timelinePlan on failing jobs

syncRootCause.perceivedBehindSpeechExplanation: Subjective 'behind' is not explained by phrase→ASS positive offset. Likely (a) speech in audio begins before segment/word timestamps (~1.79s input offset), and/or (b) full phrase visible while later words in the cue are still being spoken, and/or (c) positive assShiftSec at burn when enabled.

proof.delayAttribution: reanchorBlockTiming (firstWordStart - preroll) — negative ms, caption early not late

recommendedFixes.action: Before global sort, dedupe overlapping progressive segments (same normalized prefix) or build phrase text from owning segment.text token order; use timeline only for timing, not lexical order.
recommendedFixes.avoid: More ASS/RTL/BiDi rewrites — order is already wrong in phrase.text

recommendedFixes.action: Collapse growing partial+full cues in getSourceTruthSegments / pre-export (mirror preview segment list) so export input is one segment per utterance.
Use WHISPER-STARTTIME-FORENSICS per job to name the first function that sets segment 0 start to ~1.79s; do not adjust phrase/ASS preroll to fix late first caption.
If provider start is correct but audio speech starts earlier, fix at audio/Whisper alignment — not composeRhythmBlocks.
If assShiftSec > 0 in job timelinePlan, reduce/remove burn shift; otherwise treat as upstream timestamp vs heard speech, not phrase pipeline lag.
Audit jobs with driftCorrectionApplied > 0 for positive start corrections on early cues only.
